title: agrostis stolonifera var. elata
intro: Agrostis stolonifera var. elata, commonly known as Creeping Bentgrass, is a type of perennial grass that belongs to the Poaceae family. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine for its various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The plant is said to offer several health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and supporting digestive health. Creeping Bentgrass can be prepared into herbal remedies like teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are often used to treat various health issues, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness.
